market_metadata.title: MetaDAO: Double Developer Grant Pool to 1M USDC
market_metadata.proposal: Increase the annual developer grant pool from 500k to 1M USDC by allocating additional treasury reserves.

github_grants.reasoning: GitHub shows only 2 commits in 6 weeks with 1 active contributor. Insufficient development velocity for a doubled grant.

onchain_grant_wallet.reasoning: Previous grant wallet still holds 40% of original allocation unspent after 4 months.

telegram_dev.reasoning: Small developer Telegram (180 members). Moderate enthusiasm but controlled by grant recipient.

twitter_metadao.reasoning: MetaDAO community Twitter skeptical; several influential voters publicly opposed.

bull_case: Developer advocates argue the slow commit pace reflects deliberate architecture planning rather than inactivity. [CITATION:telegram_dev] The team claims a major refactor will unlock higher velocity. [CITATION:twitter_metadao] Some community members note that previous grant deliverables were technically sound, just behind schedule.
bear_case: On-chain evidence is damning. [CITATION:onchain_grant_wallet] 40% of the prior grant remains unspent after 4 months — doubling the grant without accountability is poor capital allocation. [CITATION:github_grants] GitHub shows 2 commits in 6 weeks with a single contributor, far below the 10+ commits/week seen in successful MetaDAO grants. MetaDAO voters have rejected similar under-performing grant renewals 5 of the last 6 times. [CITATION:twitter_metadao]
manipulation_flags: Telegram channel controlled by grant recipient — positive sentiment unreliable

position.rationale: Unspent prior grant and negligible commit activity make doubling unlikely to pass. Bear case has verifiable support; bull case relies on unsubstantiated future claims. Confidence 0.74 → 3% size (medium tier).

weaknesses: Architecture planning phases genuinely produce fewer commits
Community vote could still surprise if whales coordinate
counter_arguments: If the team ships a major demo before vote close, YES could rally quickly
Unspent grant funds could indicate prudent saving rather than inactivity
